page: Microsoft Azure F16s is an FS family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, this instance is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, consistently rank in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations also aligns with average tier performance. Notably, text generation using a small 135M parameter LLM model registers in the top 25% tier, while larger model inference and standard database operations remain in the average tier.

The F16s instance offers a compute-optimized hardware profile where the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio reduces memory-associated overhead costs. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for parallelizable workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the primary tradeoff lies in its weak single-threaded performance, meaning workloads relying heavily on single-core execution speeds may experience bottlenecks.

This server configuration is suited for multi-threaded processing tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations. It is also applicable for small-scale language model text generation and parallelized batch processing where multi-core scaling can compensate for lower single-core speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure F16s is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the FS family, powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores, 32.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core), and 66 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark evaluations show weak single-core performance, but average multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Notably, text generation with small language models performs in the top 25% tier. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, parallelized workloads by minimizing memory overhead. This instance is suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16s is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16s is an x86_64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a low memory-to-CPU ratio for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL physical cores on x86_64 architecture
32.0 GB of system memory and 66 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Weak single-core performance offset by strong small-model LLM text generation
Cost-efficient 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio minimizing memory overhead
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch processing
